stories.title: How Xiaomi’s push into AI, chips and EVs is future-proofing its hardware empire
stories.summary: Xiaomi is betting on artificial intelligence to future-proof its sprawling hardware empire, pouring massive resources into open-source models to ensure its next generation of smartphones and electric vehicles (EVs) does not get left behind in an AI-driven market. The Beijing-based firm’s latest AI model, MiMo-V2.5-Pro, introduced last month, was ranked by third-party benchmark platform Artificial

stories.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
stories.summary: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

stories.title: Oil prices fall 5% after Trump says Iran talks proceeding in a 'constructive manner'
stories.summary: Trump had said an agreement with Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, among other issues, was largely negotiated and would be announced soon.

stories.title: Gold Jumps as Prospects of Iran Deal Temper Inflation Concerns
stories.summary: Gold jumped as signs the US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz tempered inflation concerns.

stories.title: Trump says US will not ‘rush into a deal’ with Iran as talks continue
stories.summary: Price of oil falls on hopes of an agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: European Gas Declines on Optimism US and Iran Will Reach Deal
stories.summary: European natural gas dropped during early trading in Asia on optimism that the US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Stocks Rise, Oil Falls on Iran Deal Expectations: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks climbed and crude oil fell after US officials signaled that a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows may be near.

stories.title: Oil Drops as US Touts Progress on Deal Toward Reopening Strait
stories.summary: Oil dropped as the US and Iran edged toward a deal, although President Donald Trump said that Washington’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would remain until an agreement was completed.

stories.title: Chinese Firms Speed Up Plans to Build New Coal Power Plants: GEM
stories.summary: Chinese firms are accelerating the pace at which they propose new coal-fired power plants, even as the government moves to rein in growth after the rapid expansion in recent years.

stories.title: ECB Likely to Revise Its Inflation Outlook in June, Lagarde Says
stories.summary: The European Central Bank is set to lift its inflation outlook when policymakers meet next month, according to President Christine Lagarde.

stories.title: Hassett Says Ending Iran War May Create Room for Fed Rate Cut
stories.summary: Kevin Hassett, President Donald Trump’s chief economic adviser at the White House, signaled he’s confident that an eventual drop in oil prices will create space for the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates.

stories.title: Supertanker With Iraq Crude Exits Persian Gulf as Talks Continue
stories.summary: A supertanker hauling Iraqi crude to China has left the Persian Gulf and crossed the US blockade line into the Arabian Sea, as talks continue to end the war between the US and Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Pope Leo Focusing on AI in First Encyclical
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV’s launch of his first encyclical focusing on artificial intelligence next week will feature an unexpected guest: Christopher Olah, co-founder of Anthropic. Bloomberg News reporter Flavia Rotondi joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss how Pope Leo has picked AI as a key issue in his pontificate. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Rubio Sees Good News Coming on Hormuz as Iran Talks Continue
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said there may be “some good news” regarding the blocked Strait of Hormuz in the coming hours, as Iran and Washington press ahead with peace negotiations. stories.title: US Inches Toward Iran Deal as Negotiators Focus on Hormuz
stories.summary: The US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, senior US officials said Sunday, even as President Donald Trump insisted he would not “rush” into an agreement.

stories.title: Rubio Says "Significant Progress" Made on Iran Talks

stories.summary: While he says that Washington will not rush into a deal, energy stockpiles risk hitting the red zone this summer.

stories.title: Japan’s Nikkei 225 tops 65,000 for first time as oil falls on Hormuz reopening hopes
stories.summary: Japan’s Nikkei 225 breached 64,000 for the first time Monday as a sharp decline in oil prices lifted risk sentiment in holiday-thinned trading.

stories.title: Rich environments: Hong Kong banks pour money into wealth centres in pricey premises
stories.summary: HSBC Holdings and its subsidiary Hang Seng Bank, Standard Chartered Bank and China Citic Bank International (CNCBI) are among the major lenders rushing to open luxury wealth centres in Hong Kong to capture high-net-worth customers. HSBC last week opened its fifth such centre, on the 58th floor of Two International Finance Centre, with 13,000 sq ft and 34 meeting rooms with harbour views. This is i

stories.title: Indian billionaires buy foreign companies as growth slows at home
stories.summary: India Inc spent $18bn on global buyouts in 2025 and the deal value could cross $15bn in the first half of 2026.

stories.title: Indonesia to Update Markets on Commodity Export Control
stories.summary: Indonesia’s Trade Vice Minister Dyah Roro Esti Widya Putri says the government is finalizing a centralized export agency for palm oil, coal, and ferroalloys. Speaking to Bloomberg’s Stephen Engle at APEC in China, she stated the plan is moving to execution despite policy shifts that have unnerved investors. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Dubious Chinese Carbon Credits Expose European Market’s Flaws
stories.summary: Companies in at least nine European countries bought carbon offsets from projects that appear not to exist or were verified by an auditor-turned-developer who treated the two jobs as a revolving door, a Bloomberg investigation found.

stories.title: Japan Regulator Urges Firms to Use Cash for Growth, Not Returns
stories.summary: Japan’s financial regulator is urging the country’s listed companies to spend more of their cash piles on long-term business investment instead of rewarding shareholders with buybacks and higher dividends.

stories.title: New Zealand to Ease Golden Visa Investment Criteria to Allow Philanthropy
stories.summary: New Zealand will let wealthy migrants count philanthropic donations toward the investment requirement of its most popular golden visa category, widening a program that has attracted billions of dollars in pledged investment.

stories.title: Trump not rushing Iran deal, whacks critics as 'losers'
stories.summary: The president said the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports would remain in effect until "an agreement is reached, certified, and signed."

stories.title: Paz Says Protests Test Democracy Ahead of Talks
stories.summary: Bolivia President Rodrigo Paz tells Wall Street Week why pragmatism — not ideology — will define his government, and why closing the chapter on 20 years of one-party rule won’t be easy. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Republican hardliners warn Trump is giving up too much in Iran talks
stories.summary: US president is trying to quell an internal backlash from hawks who demand Tehran’s complete surrender

stories.title: Energy Prices Pose Challenge for Travelers
stories.summary: The Iran war, the Trump administration's policies on immigration, energy-price volatility and economic uncertainties have added wrinkles to travel plans for many consumers in 2026. Yet BI's proprietary survey data show resiliency in discretionary spending, at least for now, despite shifting preferences for how they plan to spend on travel and leisure activities. stories.title: Ebola Spreads Across Congo as Flights Halted, Supplies Run Low
stories.summary: Congo suspended flights to the eastern city of Bunia and regional health ministers warned of escalating cross-border risks from Ebola as the outbreak spread across three provinces and overwhelmed contact-tracing efforts. Bloomberg Health and Consumer Reporter Janice Kew joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: The Middle East war is testing the Gulf's ambitions to become an AI hub
stories.summary: Attacks on data centers in the Middle East and persistently high energy prices have altered the calculus for operators of the facilities, analysts say.

stories.title: White House Signals Imminent Deal Announcement with Iran

(Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: ECB Convenes Banks to Fix Flaws Exposed by AI Models, FT Says
stories.summary: The European Central Bank plans to press lenders to accelerate efforts to secure their IT systems after organizing a meeting on cybersecurity risks with the latest artificial intelligence models, the Financial Times reported.

stories.summary: Disney's "The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied and estimated $82 million in domestic ticket sales through its first three days in theaters.

stories.summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings Ltd. is nearing an agreement to buy I-MED Radiology Network Ltd., people familiar with the deal said on Sunday, an acquisition that would add Australia’s biggest medical diagnostic imaging provider to the conglomerate’s portfolio.

stories.title: Latest ‘Star Wars’ Film ‘Mandalorian’ Debuts at $102 Million
stories.summary: The Mandalorian and Grogu, the first film in the Star Wars franchise in seven years, was the highest-grossing movie in the US and Canada over the four-day holiday weekend with $102 million in ticket sales.

stories.title: College Kids Don’t Want Your AI
stories.summary: The unique threat artificial intelligence poses to students’ education and their hopes of someday landing a job has made universities a powerful crucible for AI resistance. Students are pushing back against the adoption of AI on campuses, with some organizing protests, petitions, and performance art to express their concerns about the technology's effects on their education and job prospects. stories.title: Nigeria’s Tinubu Secures Nomination for 2027 Presidential Vote
stories.summary: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu further strengthened his hand as the ruling party’s top candidate in next year’s presidential elections after winning overwhelming support in primaries against sole challenger Stanley Osifo.

stories.title: Rep. Ivey "Disgusted" at Settlement Slush Fund, Critiques $1B Ballroom Budget Request
stories.summary: Rep. Glenn Ivey (D-MD) joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend for a wide-ranging conversation on a possible deal with Iran, the anti-weaponization settlement and more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Trump Mulls Arms Sale to Taiwan, Will Speak to President
stories.summary: President Donald Trump indicated he would speak directly with Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te after pausing a $14 billion sale of arms to the country in a move that would risk disrupting newly stabilized ties between the US and China. Ambassador Alexander Yui, Taiwan's representative to the U.S. joined David Gura, Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Americans are 'entrenched' in financial stress amid debt and price pressures
stories.summary: Inflation and high debt levels continue cause financial anxiety for many Americans. Nonprofits can help with debt mangement.

stories.title: How New Mega-Cap IPO's Will Reshape the Markets
stories.summary: SpaceX is marketing itself to IPO investors as an artificial intelligence targeting a $26.5 trillion potential market opportunity. The move signaling its intention to wrest business from investor darlings whose valuations have soared. Bloomberg Market Structure Research Head Larry Tabb joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: I’ve worked with over 1,000 kids—the ones with the best people skills use these 6 phrases
stories.summary: "Strong people skills are not the same as people-pleasing," says child life specialist and therapist Kelsey Mora, who's supported thousands of kids and families.

stories.title: Resurgent Political Risk Derails Rallies Across Emerging Markets
stories.summary: Investors in emerging markets are getting slammed by a fresh wave of political turmoil that is derailing bets from Latin America to Eastern Europe.

stories.title: Gunman Killed by Secret Service After Firing Near White House
stories.summary: Shortly after 6pm Saturday a 21-year-old man approached a checkpoint near 17th Street and Pennsylvania Avenue and opened fire on Secret Service officers. The agents returned fire, striking the gunman, who was transported to a local hospital and later died. Lisa Mateo on Bloomberg This Weekend gives the latest update on the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Move over, seltzer. Non-carbonated drinks are taking the spotlight

stories.summary: Hong Kong is likely to attract more mainland Chinese retail brands, but unlike the previous wave, non food and beverage (F&B) retailers are the ones ramping up their presence, according to analysts. The change in profile of mainland brands coming to Hong Kong is helping the city’s landlords fill vacant spaces amid an uneven recovery in the retail property market, according to JLL. stories.title: Hungary’s New Premier Warns Predecessor Left Budget ‘Skeletons’
stories.summary: Hungary’s new Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he was hoping for economic growth of about 2% this year but put voters on notice that the state of public finances left by his predecessor made it impossible to make firm forecasts.

stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people protested in Serbia’s capital against the government, seeking to keep up pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic even after he backed holding an early election in the fall.
